column2: Aqualogica Glow+ Dewy Sunscreen SPF 50+ PA++++
column17:
• Use every morning as the last step before makeup or outdoor exposure.
• Apply generously to all exposed areas, including face, neck, ears, and hands.
• Reapply every 2-3 hours when outdoors, sweating, or after towel-drying.
• Use even on cloudy days and when sitting near windows for long periods.
• Let it settle for a few minutes before applying makeup.
column18:
• Do not rely on a thin layer for full protection.
• Do not skip reapplication during prolonged sun exposure.
• Do not apply too close to the eyes if it causes watering or stinging.
• Do not use an expired product, especially for outdoor protection.
• Do not mix directly with moisturiser or foundation in your palm if it reduces even coverage.
column19:
• Close the cap or lid tightly after every use.
• Store in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight, heat, and bathroom steam.
• Keep water out of the container and avoid touching the opening unnecessarily.
• Keep away from children and pets.
• Check smell, colour, texture, and expiry before use; discard if anything seems unusual.
• Avoid leaving it in hot cars, window sills, or direct sunlight for long hours.
column20:
• Use in the morning after moisturiser and before makeup.
• Take enough quantity to cover face and neck evenly.
• Spread in small sections until there are no missed patches.
• Allow it to settle before stepping out.
• Reapply during the day when exposed to sun, heat, sweat, or water.
column21: Key ingredient focus: Vitamin C; secondary ingredients: Papaya Extract. Vitamin C can tingle on compromised skin; use in the morning with sunscreen or at night if skin is sensitive. Sunscreen filters should be used as directed and reapplied; avoid direct eye contact and rinse if stinging occurs. Patch test behind the ear or jawline for 24-48 hours if sensitive, allergy-prone, pregnant/breastfeeding, using prescription treatments, or recovering from procedures. This is cosmetic guidance and not a substitute for dermatologist advice for eczema, rosacea, melasma, active infection, or severe acne.

column17:
• Start with a small amount and apply only on clean, dry skin.
• Use at a low frequency at first, then increase only if skin stays comfortable.
• Follow with moisturiser to reduce dryness or tightness.
• Use sunscreen in the morning routine when using targeted treatments.

column18:
